home.title: Begin learning, writing, and building your record right away.
home.subtitle: Neofolk Atlas is a calm learning platform for courses, notes, projects, and groups. You can start within seconds and grow into the deeper system later.
home.languageTitle: Use the platform in the language that feels easiest
home.signupNote: Every new account begins as a Seeker. Curator access is applied for later.
home.loginNote: Return to your courses, notes, and progress.
home.stepsTitle: What you can do right now
home.step1Body: Open Learn, choose a topic, and begin with one structured course instead of everything at once.
home.step2Body: Notes help you remember ideas, questions, and observations while you learn.
home.step3Body: Projects, essays, and documentation become your visible academic record over time.
home.philosophyTitle: The system is deep, but it does not need to feel complicated on day one.
home.philosophyBody: Neofolk keeps the original intellectual structure of Seekers, Curators, Arbiters, Guilds, and Discovery, but it reveals that complexity gradually as your learning record grows.
home.termCoursesBody: Structured pathways for guided learning. In the deeper system, these live inside Modules and Subjects.
home.termGroupsBody: Learning groups organized around shared interests, inquiry, and collaboration.
home.termProjectsBody: Your essays, designs, research, and documentation become a portfolio over time.

messages.checkEmail: Signup successful. Check your email to confirm your account before logging in.
messages.unverifiedEmail: Unverified email. Please confirm your email first.
messages.noProfile: No profile found for this account.
messages.enterEmailForReset: Enter your email first to reset your password.
messages.passwordResetSent: Password reset email sent if the account exists.
messages.accountCreated: Account created. You can now continue into the Atlas.
messages.locationUnavailableBody: This browser does not support location access.
messages.locationSavedBody: Nearby module search is now enabled. When module locations are available, search results will show the closest options first.
messages.locationDeniedBody: Allow location access to see nearby modules.

search.subject: Subject
search.noResultsBody: No matching records for this query.

onboarding.title: How to use the platform
onboarding.step1Title: Begin in the simplest possible way
onboarding.step1Note: You should be able to start learning without understanding the whole philosophy first.
onboarding.step1Card1: Choose the interface language that feels easiest before doing anything else.
onboarding.step1Card2: Start from the topics you care about. The platform becomes deeper after your interests are clear.
onboarding.step1Card3: Pick one course and begin there. Do not try to understand every area at once.
onboarding.step2Title: Use the platform in a calm order
onboarding.step2Note: The first layer is intentionally practical: learn, write, build, and track progress.
onboarding.step2Card1: Learn is where you find structured study paths and available topics.
onboarding.step2Card2: Notes are for questions, observations, and ideas you do not want to lose.
onboarding.step2Card3: Projects turn your strongest work into a visible academic record.
onboarding.step2Card4: Progress helps you see your current course, next step, and learning rhythm.
onboarding.step3Note: Original Neofolk terminology stays intact, but you do not need to master it on your first day.
onboarding.step3Card1: Subjects are structured learning areas that help you know what to study next.
onboarding.step3Card2: Groups, or Guilds, are for shared inquiry and collaboration once you are ready.
onboarding.step3Card3: The Dictionary explains each original term in simple language whenever you need it.
onboarding.step3Card4: The Help page gives plain-language guidance for every major section of the platform.
onboarding.step4Title: Use support tools when they help
onboarding.step4Note: The platform should stay useful on mobile, across languages, and under limited connectivity.
onboarding.step4Card1: Use the language selector in the header or on the homepage to switch the interface directly.
onboarding.step4Card2: Use Near Me to surface the closest module options when location data is available.
onboarding.step4Card3: Discovery, deeper Guild identity, and Curator pathways appear gradually after stronger engagement.

dashboard.title: Your learning dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Start from your current course, write notes, build projects, and let the deeper system appear only when it becomes useful.
dashboard.noCourseBody: Browse topics and begin with one course that feels most relevant right now.
dashboard.nextProjectBody: Turn your best recent work into a project entry so your progress stays visible.
dashboard.firstProjectBody: Start with one simple reflection, project, or documented exercise.
dashboard.resumeCourseBody: Return to your active course and keep moving in a steady rhythm.
dashboard.pickCourseBody: Begin with one course rather than exploring everything at once.
dashboard.noNotesBody: Write short notes while you learn so the platform becomes useful immediately.
dashboard.projectCardBody: Save your strongest work here. Keep it simple and intentional.
dashboard.noteCardBody: Use notes for ideas, questions, and reminders while you are learning.
dashboard.suggestedTagsTitle: Topics you seem to care about
dashboard.noSuggestedTags: Suggested tags appear as you write notes and projects.
dashboard.noRecommendedCourseBody: Recommendations appear as more courses and tags become available.
dashboard.groupsAdvancedBody: groups are part of your deeper learning layer.
dashboard.groupsAdvancedEmpty: Groups appear later once you are ready for collaborative inquiry.
dashboard.discoveryAdvancedBody: Highlighted work and published visibility appear after stronger engagement.
dashboard.curatorAdvancedBody: Curator access is applied for later, after learning evidence and seriousness are visible.

dictionary.title: Neofolk terms, explained simply
dictionary.subtitle: Original terminology stays intact, but every key word is explained in plain language.

seeker.label: Seeker
seeker.simple: Student building an intellectual record through study.
seeker.expanded: A Seeker is the default learner role. Seekers study courses, write notes, build projects, and gradually enter deeper layers like groups and portfolio identity.
seeker.example: A Seeker starts by taking one course and writing one note.

curator.label: Curator
curator.simple: Teacher who creates structured learning modules.
curator.expanded: Curators guide learning by designing modules, clarifying expected outputs, and helping learners move through knowledge in a structured way.
curator.example: A Curator prepares a module on ecology or history.

arbiter.label: Arbiter
arbiter.expanded: Arbiters review modules, approvals, studio access, and highlighted work so the platform remains serious and academically credible.
arbiter.example: An Arbiter approves a module before it becomes visible.

guild.label: Guild
guild.simple: Learning group organized around shared intellectual interests.
guild.expanded: A Guild is a collaborative research group where people investigate, discuss, and produce work together around related topics.
guild.example: A Guild may gather people interested in ecology, music, or craft.

module.label: Module
module.expanded: A Module gives a learner an organized path through a subject, including guidance, direction, and expected outputs.
module.example: A Module may cover a focused area inside Lingosophy or Praxis.

discovery.label: Discovery
discovery.simple: Public showcase of notable academic work.
discovery.expanded: Discovery is where highlighted work appears after review. It is meant to feel like a calm academic journal, not a social feed.
discovery.example: A strong project may appear in Discovery after review.

praxis.label: Praxis
praxis.simple: Learning through disciplined practice and action.
praxis.expanded: Praxis refers to embodied learning, physical discipline, and practical action as a serious part of education.
praxis.example: Movement, field practice, and disciplined craft can all belong to Praxis.

lingosophy.label: Lingosophy
lingosophy.simple: Study of language, thought, and meaning.
lingosophy.expanded: Lingosophy is one of Neofolk's subject areas. It covers language, interpretation, communication, and the way words shape understanding.
lingosophy.example: A learner studying literature or meaning systems may begin with Lingosophy.
